commands.review_summary:
The PR refactors `getStatusSummary` to collect lightweight session candidates, hydrate only the recent aggregate and per-agent rows, and adds regression coverage for the hydration boundary.

Reproducibility: yes. for the source-level path: current `main` hydrates every stored session before slicing to recent rows, matching the linked status-latency report. I did not run a live before/after timing reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: No repair lane is needed: this is already an open focused implementation PR with supplied proof and no discrete review finding for automation to fix.

target.title: fix(status): limit session summary hydration
target.body:
## Summary
- collect lightweight session candidates first, then hydrate only the recent rows rendered by status summaries
- preserve total and per-agent session counts from the full store
- add regression coverage proving non-recent rows are not model/runtime hydrated

Fixes #84787

## Real behavior proof
Behavior or issue addressed: Status summary generation should keep full total/per-agent session counts while avoiding model/runtime/context hydration for stored sessions that are not returned in recent status rows.

Real environment tested: Real local OpenClaw setup on macOS, running the patched PR branch against my actual config and session stores. The store contains 634 sessions across 6 agent stores, including one agent store with 520 sessions. Runtime version reported by the patched summary path: 2026.5.20.

Exact steps or command run after the patch:
1. Checked out this PR branch locally.
2. Ran the patched `getStatusSummary` implementation against the real local OpenClaw config/session stores.
3. Disabled channel summary for this proof so the measurement isolates the session-summary path changed by this PR.
4. Captured elapsed time, aggregate session count, per-agent counts, and returned recent-row counts.

Evidence after fix:
```bash
/usr/bin/time -p node --import tsx -e 'const start=Date.now(); const {getStatusSummary}=await import("./src/commands/status.summary.ts"); const s=await getStatusSummary({includeChannelSummary:false}); console.log(JSON.stringify({ok:true,elapsedMs:Date.now()-start,sessionsCount:s.sessions.count,recentCount:s.sessions.recent.length,byAgent:s.sessions.byAgent.map(a=>({agentId:a.agentId,count:a.count,recent:a.recent.length})),paths:s.sessions.paths.length,runtimeVersion:s.runtimeVersion}));'
```

```json
{"ok":true,"elapsedMs":6333,"sessionsCount":634,"recentCount":10,"byAgent":[{"agentId":"main","count":41,"recent":10},{"agentId":"ecom","count":9,"recent":9},{"agentId":"finance","count":13,"recent":10},{"agentId":"greys","count":520,"recent":10},{"agentId":"tech","count":37,"recent":10},{"agentId":"work","count":14,"recent":10}],"paths":6,"runtimeVersion":"2026.5.20"}
real 6.50
user 5.02
sys 2.48
```

Observed result after fix: The patched session-summary path completed successfully on the real 634-session store, preserved the full aggregate count and full per-agent counts, and returned only recent hydrated rows: aggregate recent=10; per-agent recent rows are capped at 10 except the 9-session `ecom` store, which returned 9. This is the behavior the PR is intended to provide before full status rendering consumes the summary.

What was not tested: I did not use the full `openclaw status` CLI as proof here because this local setup still has an additional channel-summary/status slow path unrelated to the session-summary hydration issue fixed by this PR. Channel summary was disabled deliberately to isolate the changed path.

## Verification
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/status.summary.test.ts
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/status.summary.test.ts src/commands/status.scan.fast-json.test.ts src/commands/status.scan.test.ts
- pnpm run check:test-types
- pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/commands/status.summary.ts src/commands/status.summary.test.ts
- git diff --check
- pnpm run lint:core
